title: serratula nudicaulis
intro: Serratula nudicaulis, also known as the Naked Milk Thistle or Naked Burnet-saxifrage, is a perennial plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Serratula nudicaulis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which have been reported to have health benefits including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting liver function.
